Logical consistency is therefore a load-bearing review concern.","R1.3":"The conclusion ('blind deployment cleanly separates content-driven from identity-driven trust') is premised on participants being unaware of routing identity, but the badge-hover instrumentation and the in-context attribution survey ('Was this response from the fast local model or the large cloud model?') both supply identity cues. Verifying whether the data actually support the stated conclusions is central.","R1.6":"Generic 'review this section' delegations standardly include prose-level assessment; writing polish is a normal expected responsibility even though the more critical issues here are methodological."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I treat this as a substantive content review — checking internal logical consistency, methodological validity, and claim-evidence alignment — or primarily as a prose/copyediting pass focused on writing quality?"}

rationale.R1.2: The section asserts a 'blind protocol' (no model identifier exposed, latency equalized) as the methodological foundation for its causal claim, yet later describes routing badges (green leaf / blue cloud) that participants hovered over before rating — a direct internal contradiction that undermines the study's core license for causal attribution. Logical consistency is therefore a load-bearing review concern.
rationale.R1.3: The conclusion ('blind deployment cleanly separates content-driven from identity-driven trust') is premised on participants being unaware of routing identity, but the badge-hover instrumentation and the in-context attribution survey ('Was this response from the fast local model or the large cloud model?') both supply identity cues. Verifying whether the data actually support the stated conclusions is central.
rationale.R1.6: Generic 'review this section' delegations standardly include prose-level assessment; writing polish is a normal expected responsibility even though the more critical issues here are methodological.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I treat this as a substantive content review — checking internal logical consistency, methodological validity, and claim-evidence alignment — or primarily as a prose/copyediting pass focused on writing quality?
